column3: If Method allowed you to transfer inventory between locations, how would you feel?
column4: If Method did NOT allow inventory transfers between locations, how would you feel?
column5: If you were clearly notified when an item was running low in Method, how would you feel?
column6: If you were not notified when an item was running low, how would you feel?
column7: If Method supported backorders when inventory is insufficient, how would you feel?
column8: If Method did NOT support backorders and required items to be in stock to sell, how would you feel?
column9: If Method allowed you to track inventory by bin or sub-location within a warehouse, how would you feel?
column10: If Method did NOT provide any bin-level visibility, how would you feel?
column11: If Method allowed you to easily reorder line items in transactions, how would you feel?
column12: If Method did NOT allow you to reorder line items, how would you feel?
column13: If Method allowed you to attach images to inventory items, how would you feel?
column14: If Method did NOT support item images, how would you feel?

column16: If Method did NOT provide visibility into inventory change history, how would you feel?
column17: If you could generate packing slips for orders being shipped, how would you feel?
column18: If you could not generate packing slips, how would you feel?
column19: If you could apply different prices for the same item based on customer, quantity, or promotion, how would you feel?
column20: If you could not apply different prices and had to use a single price per item, how would you feel?
